blocks.content: Mr. Alvarez had produced a set of small trivia cards from somewhere behind the counter — the kind with multiple categories, worn at the edges, which suggested they had been in the drawer for some time and were now being deployed for exactly the right occasion.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: One person can be the judge.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: That seems structurally unsound. He knows everything. He'll be a biased judge.

blocks.speaker: Mr. Alvarez
blocks.content: I'll be a fair judge. I've had years of practice at that too.

Nathan had already proposed teams — himself and Cassandra versus Ethan and Lena, with Maya as the wildcard — when Cassandra objected on the grounds that Nathan had seen her trivia abilities at the arcade and was strategically selecting her as a weak partner.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You watched me lose a geography round on purpose and now you want me on your team.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: I'm doing a little bit of that. But also I just wanted to be on your team.
Ethan moved around the table to sit beside her. Nathan accepted this with philosophical grace and moved across to sit next to Lena.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: The archive of our past collaboration suggests this will be competitive.

blocks.speaker: Lena
blocks.content: The archive of things you know about me is very small. Don't put too much weight on it.
He was smiling, and Lena was also smiling, and Maya at her end of the table appeared to be making a careful assessment of what was happening.
Mr. Alvarez picked up the first card.
